old_claim.note: Synthetic Ecosystem Restoration is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: Closed-loop agriculture is part of bioregenerative life support, recycling water, nutrients, waste, CO2, and plant biomass to reduce resupply for Mars habitats.

source_shape.source_locator: NTRS staged BLiSS architecture uses recycled water and nutrients to produce habitat food supply with plants, while plants provide oxygen, remove carbon dioxide, and support water purification.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA's staged BLiSS roadmap places plant food production, atmosphere regeneration, water purification, and waste/feedstock recycling inside Mars-relevant closed-loop life support.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing the broad ecosystem-restoration dependency with the life-support capability that directly supplies the water, nutrient, atmosphere, waste, and crop-production loops in the node claim.

ontology_before: The graph made Mars closed-loop agriculture depend on synthetic ecosystem restoration, suggesting AI-guided open ecosystem repair was a prerequisite for habitat food loops.
ontology_after: The graph routes Mars closed-loop agriculture through closed-loop life support and orbital greenhouse lineage, keeping the node scoped to controlled-environment food systems inside habitats.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mars_closed_loop_agriculture now depends on closed_loop_life_support and no longer directly on synthetic_ecosystem_restoration.
would_reject_if: The Mars agriculture node is later rescoped to open Martian ecosystem repair or terraforming rather than habitat food systems.
A source shows Mars closed-loop agriculture requires synthetic ecosystem restoration as a direct prerequisite instead of bioregenerative life-support and controlled-environment crop systems.
short_reason: Closed-loop life support is the direct enabling system for recycled-water, nutrient, waste, atmosphere, and crop loops in Martian habitat agriculture.
